title: Performance, Load, Supply-Chain Gates, and Production Client Defense · Browser and Node Client Sys… · Contract Lens: Load Fairness Overload
summary: A production TypeScript client must defend Core Web UX, p95 latency, bundle and memory cost, dependency gates, rollback, and changed traffic. Planned artifact: Add and verify the http ts load fairness overload increment in a TypeScript 7 standards-based Fetch production HTTP client release dossier. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Test concurrency, fairness, quotas, backpressure, retry amplification, and graceful overload under changed demand. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.

Profile allocations, buffering, decoding, connection reuse, and response retention with representative sizes. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Fetch and TypeScript Predict Load Fairness Overload · Theory Load Fairness Overload
steps.instruction:
theory case — TypeScript Fetch evidence model release T-3 doubles traffic and body size while p95 latency, memory, retries, and queue depth rise. Measure the bottleneck, preserve behavior, verify supply-chain gates and rollback, then defend production readiness. Competency probe: Test concurrency, fairness, quotas, backpressure, retry amplification, and graceful overload under changed demand. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.
